Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Supboena For Production Of Business Records Directed To Third Party G. Hensler & Co.
Off Calendar Aug-06-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff Jennifer Righetti's motion to quash subpoena for production of third party business records is granted in part. The subpoena is modified to only require production of those portions of resumes, applications, or other documents that describe plaintiff's duties while employed by defendant. The request for monetary sanctions is denied. Prevailing party to submit form of order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance With May 15, 2007 Discovery Order; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Jul-26-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff Jennifer Righetti's motion to compel compliance with prior order is granted in part. The Court will clarify the prior by striking the general objections. The Code of Civil Procedure does not authorize general objections. The remaining objections are overruled. Defendant is ordered to further supplement its response to special interrogatory #5 by specifying what facts it contends support the applicability of the administrative, executive, and professional exemptions. The current answer generally describes employee duties and the elements of particular exemptions. A further response shall be served within 10 days of notice of this order. The request for monetary sanctions is denied. Prevailing party to submit form of order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.

MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Daniel H. Qualls, Esq., for plaintiff and moving party: Jennifer B. Righetti
Tamara I. Devitt, Esq., for defendant and opposing party: Insidetrack, Inc.,

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Jennifer Righetti's motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories and request for production of documents is granted in part.
Interrogatories
1, 2
Granted. Defendant shall disclose the names of employees, subject to the parties sending a letter pursuant to Belaire-West Landscape Inc. v. Superior Court.

5,7
Granted.

Request for production of documents
Granted. Defendant may redact the names and other personal identifying information of the employees.

Supplemental responses shall be served within 15 days of notice of this order. Defendant's request for monetary sanctions is denied. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.
